description: You are a cost engineer at a manufacturing company. Management needs lifecycle material cost reports for all assembled products in the AdventureWorks2022 database. Build a multi-level BOM cost reporting system that includes a recursive view 'dbo.vw_ProductBOMHierarchy' to fully traverse active rows (EndDate IS NULL) in Production.BillOfMaterials, exposing assembly and component identities, names, hierarchy depth, quantity, and unit of measure. The system must also include a cost summary table 'Production.LifecycleCostSummary' (with a non-clustered index on AssemblyProductID) that stores assembly identities, total component counts across all levels, maximum hierarchy depth, direct material costs (level 1 components only), full BOM material costs (all levels), and report timestamps. Implement and execute a stored procedure 'dbo.usp_GenerateBOMCostReport' that populates the summary table by querying the hierarchy view and computing the required cost metrics. The final implementation must demonstrate multi-level traversal and contain populated cost data for at least five assemblies.

metadata.application: Azure Data Studio + Microsoft SQL Server 2022
metadata.dataset: AdventureWorks2022 Production schema (Microsoft sample database with multi-level product BOM data)
metadata.difficulty_rationale: Requires a recursive CTE to traverse multi-level BOM hierarchy, correct anchor/recursive member construction, multiple aggregation windows (direct vs full BOM cost), stored procedure with TRUNCATE+INSERT pattern, non-clustered index creation, and cross-joining Production.Product twice for assembly and component names.
